tweets.text:
i think the thing i have never liked about alot of crypto culture in general is the zero sum mentality that most people have.  i can't tell you how many times someone has been posting/telling me about their dream thesis on coin playing out, only to find out they got out soon after.

when i was sitting on a trading desk, that's the type of thing that would get you kicked off the desk.

tweets.text:
Previously done Olympic weightlifting, powerlifting and then sport specific training (lots of power based work basically i guess with mimics of the sports above)

Messed around with a bit of calisthenics stuff in the past too because being able to actually move my body is probs a good thing

now just lift for fun / maintaining muscle on a Push Pull Legs split 6 days a week. Travel a lot and much easier to maintain than the strength sports across variety of gyms etc

recommend mainly just trying lots of stuff, learning why and how it's effective and for what specific goals then finding a combination of what you enjoy best crossed with your goals, only way to be sustainable without hating the experience imo
tweets.full_text:
Previously done Olympic weightlifting, powerlifting and then sport specific training (lots of power based work basically i guess with mimics of the sports above)

Messed around with a bit of calisthenics stuff in the past too because being able to actually move my body is probs a good thing

now just lift for fun / maintaining muscle on a Push Pull Legs split 6 days a week. Travel a lot and much easier to maintain than the strength sports across variety of gyms etc

recommend mainly just trying lots of stuff, learning why and how it's effective and for what specific goals then finding a combination of what you enjoy best crossed with your goals, only way to be sustainable without hating the experience imo
